articles.title: GOOGLE ROLLS OUT NANO BANANA 2 FOR FASTER IMAGES
articles.summary: Google has made Nano Banana 2—powered by Gemini 3.1 Flash—its default image generator across Gemini, Search, Flow, AI Studio, and the API, delivering faster, sharper images with much stronger text rendering and instruction following. It improves consistency across characters and objects, boosts realism and layout accuracy, adds production-ready controls (aspect ratio, up to 4K), and shows more reliable results for iterative creative workflows like posters, product visuals, and multi-scene character designs.

articles.title: NEW STUDIO DISPLAY MAY HAVE HIGHER-END MODEL WITH THESE UPGRADES, PER LEAK
articles.summary: Rumors and leaked Apple code suggest Apple is preparing two new Studio Display models, possibly launching as soon as next week, with at least one featuring ProMotion, HDR, and an A19 chip. The higher-end version may include a larger display, more powerful speakers, expanded I/O, and potentially 120Hz support (while a lower-end model could offer 90Hz), though exact sizes and feature splits remain unclear.

articles.title: INTRODUCING CONTRA PAYMENTS
articles.summary: Contra has launched Contra Payments, a commission-free platform helping over 1.2M creatives — who've already earned $200M — get paid through invoices, payment links, digital products, and structured projects. The platform replaced its flat $29 contract fee with a scaled fee model based on transaction size, with Pro and Max membership tiers reducing or eliminating client-facing platform fees. Contra acts as the merchant of record for digital product sales, handling tax collection and disputes, while payout fees vary by processor and destination country.

articles.title: WHY GENERATIVE AI IS BAD FOR DESIGN
articles.summary: Generative AI tools—especially chat-based ones—conflict with core design thinking, which is visual, spatial, non-linear, and exploratory rather than sequential and output-driven. Designers should use AI intentionally to support sketching, comparison, and divergent exploration, not replace them, or risk shifting from true design thinking to simply prompting for polished deliverables.

articles.title: YOUR USERS AREN'T HUMAN ANYMORE; START BUILDING FOR AGENTS TODAY
articles.summary: Traditional SaaS metrics like DAU/MAU are becoming misleading as AI agents increasingly act on behalf of users, shifting value creation from human logins to programmatic actions. Companies should track Monthly Active Agents (MAA), focus on agent retention as the new signal of product-market fit, redesign products for programmatic access (API/MCP-first), and move beyond seat-based pricing toward usage- or value-based models in an agent-driven software economy.

articles.title: WHY SOFT SKILLS WILL DEFINE DESIGN CAREERS OVER THE NEXT TWO YEARS
articles.summary: Soft skills, particularly communication, will define design careers over the next two years as AI democratizes basic design creation. Many designers fail to truly communicate by only informing stakeholders rather than engaging in two-way dialogue and explaining their design decisions. As AI tools can now generate polished interfaces quickly, a designer's value lies in becoming a consultative partner who can articulate what makes design good versus mediocre.

articles.title: BUILD REAL APPS, NOT PROTOTYPES
articles.summary: Modelence is a full-stack platform for building production-ready apps — with authentication, database, and deployment built in.

articles.title: EDIT YOUR FRONT-END, VISUALLY
articles.summary: Inspector is a visual front-end editor that connects to your AI coding agent (Cursor, Claude Code, or Codex). Edit text, move elements, and iterate on your React, Next.js, or Vite app—all locally on your codebase.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S PIXEL COLOR CHART OFFERS A FASCINATING GLIMPSE AT SMARTPHONE DESIGN TRENDS
articles.summary: To celebrate 10 years of the Pixel, Google released an infographic showcasing every Pixel model's color palette since 2016, highlighting how the brand has consistently embraced bold hues. With the Pixel 10 lineup offering ten color options, the chart reflects a broader trend toward more playful smartphone design—though some argue color variety may be compensating for slowing hardware innovation.

articles.title: WHAT IS A FULL-STACK CONTENT DESIGNER?
articles.summary: As AI reshapes content design roles, simply branding yourself a “generalist” may not be enough, since breadth without depth risks positioning you as a jack of all trades. Instead, the stronger survival strategy is becoming a “full-stack” (comb-shaped) content designer — someone with multiple content specialisms across research, strategy, writing, and operations — enabling adaptability, deeper value, and greater resilience in a changing job market.

articles.title: GAS TOWN'S AGENT PATTERNS, DESIGN BOTTLENECKS, AND VIBECODING AT SCALE
articles.summary: When agents write all the code, design, and planning become the primary bottlenecks in software development. Steve Yegge's Gas Town is an experimental agent orchestrator that runs dozens of coding agents simultaneously, despite being inefficient and expensive, revealing emerging patterns for future agent-based development workflows. Though not practical for current developers, it serves as valuable speculative design fiction that explores how agentic coding systems will reshape software engineering.

articles.title: DESIGNING FOR TIME: CREATING INTERFACES THAT TELL STORIES THROUGH DATA
articles.summary: Designing effective interfaces requires showing data progression over time rather than static snapshots, as users understand information through patterns and changes.

articles.title: IS CANVA'S NEW ACQUISITION A SECRET BLESSING FOR MOTION DESIGNERS?
articles.summary: Canva recently acquired motion design software Cavalry and AI video company MangoAI, expanding its professional creative suite to challenge Adobe's dominance.
